title: gilia multicaulis subsp. nevinii
intro: Gilia multicaulis subsp. nevinii, also known as Nevin's gilia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Nevin's gilia has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Nevin's gilia can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
